title: Dashboard UI
description: React app: session tree (infinite nesting), intent graph visualization (nodes=service+intent, edges=relationships), node click to raw token viewer, approval queue with approve/reject + countdown, activity feed via WebSocket.

title: React scaffold + layout
description: React app with Tailwind + shadcn/ui. Three-panel layout: session tree (left), intent graph (center), detail/tokens (right). WebSocket connection to /ws.

title: Session tree view
description: Infinite-nesting tree of active agent sessions. Each node shows: role badge, task summary, state indicator. Click to focus. Real-time updates via WebSocket agent:spawn/agent:complete events.

title: Intent graph visualization
description: Center panel: directed graph of IntentNodes. Nodes labeled service+intent (e.g. 'Gmail: Triaging Support Requests'). Edges show relationships (triggers, informs, depends_on). Live updates via intent:update WebSocket events. Use a graph library (e.g. react-flow, d3-force).

title: Raw token viewer
description: Right panel: clicking an intent node shows the raw tokens for that token_range. Categorized display: thinking blocks, tool calls with args/results, response text. Syntax highlighting for code.

title: Approval queue UI
description: Approval cards with: tool name, args summary, risk level badge, agent's justification, approve/reject buttons, countdown timer. Receives approval:required via WebSocket, sends approval:respond back.

title: Skills + Demo Polish
description: Skills loader (parse SKILL.md), night-out SKILL.md, end-to-end integration testing, demo run-through and bug fixes.

title: Skills loader
description: nightowl/skills/loader.py: load_skills(dir) parses SKILL.md files (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). build_skills_prompt(skills) formats as XML for system prompt injection. Only main agents get skills.

title: Night-out demo skill
description: nightowl/skills_library/night-out/SKILL.md: Skill for planning nights out. Instructions for: checking calendar, searching restaurants, comparing options, making reservations. Demonstrates parallel child spawning.

title: End-to-end integration test
description: Full demo flow: Telegram message -> plan a night out -> parallel children (calendar check + restaurant search) -> HITL approval for reservation -> response back on Telegram. Dashboard shows intent graph live. Verify all channels, sandboxes, HITL, observability.

title: Gateway + Dashboard API
description: REST router (ingest, sessions, health, approve, tokens, intent-graph endpoints), WebSocket connection manager, broadcast wiring from SessionManager events. The API surface the dashboard consumes.

comments.author: francois
comments.text: Skills upload: Users need to add/import custom skills.md files from the dashboard UI. Backend needs a POST /api/v1/skills endpoint to accept .md uploads and store them. The prompt builder already accepts skills_prompt — wire it to load from stored skills. This is key for non-technical users who can't SSH in to drop files.

title: REST router
description: nightowl/gateway/router.py: POST /api/v1/message/ingest, GET /sessions, GET /sessions/{id}, GET /sessions/{id}/tokens, GET /sessions/{id}/intent-graph, POST /sessions/{id}/approve, GET /health.

title: WebSocket connection manager
description: nightowl/gateway/websocket.py: ConnectionManager class. connect(), disconnect(), broadcast(event_type, payload). Events: agent:thinking, agent:tool_call, agent:response, agent:spawn, agent:complete, approval:required, approval:timeout, intent:update, error. Client sends: approval:respond.

title: Wire SessionManager broadcasts
description: Hook SessionManager lifecycle events into WebSocket broadcasts. On spawn -> agent:spawn, on complete -> agent:complete, on tool call -> agent:tool_call, etc.

title: Intent Graph Observability
description: Token store (append-only per session), intent classifier (Haiku classifies token chunks into service+intent nodes), intent graph builder (live graph maintenance), WebSocket intent:update events, wire token capture into session runner.

comments.author: francois
comments.text: Shadow agent pattern: When a user asks questions about an agent's current or previous work, spin up a 'shadow agent' — an identical agent (same model, same system prompt, same message history) but WITHOUT tools. The user chats with it via a dashboard endpoint scoped to a session. If the shadow determines a correction is needed, it calls sessions_send to steer the live agent. The main agent is never interrupted or context-polluted unless a correction is warranted. Implementation needs: (1) snapshot main agent's message history, (2) toolless Pydantic AI agent instantiation, (3) dashboard chat endpoint scoped to a session, (4) sessions_send bridge back to the live agent.

title: Token store
description: nightowl/observability/token_store.py: TokenStore class. Append-only per session. append(session_id, token_type, content) where token_type is thinking|tool_call|tool_result|response. get_range(session_id, start, end) for node drill-down. Wire into session runner to capture all agent output.

title: Intent classifier
description: nightowl/observability/intent_classifier.py: Background task that chunks token stream by tool call boundaries. Calls Haiku to classify each chunk into {service, intent, status, relationship}. Returns IntentNode + IntentEdge.

title: Intent graph builder
description: nightowl/observability/intent_graph.py: IntentGraphManager. Maintains live IntentGraph per session. upsert_node(), add_edge(). On update, broadcasts intent:update via WebSocket. get_graph(session_id) returns current graph for REST endpoint.

title: Review and test core engine prompts
description: Spike: Review all system prompts in prompt_builder.py against OpenClaw's prompt patterns. Test with real Bedrock calls to verify: (1) main agent correctly spawns children and waits, (2) orchestrator passes through tasks correctly, (3) leaf agent completes work without attempting to spawn. Validate no-poll instruction is respected. Check prompt quality for edge cases like empty skills, deep nesting, mixed sandbox modes.

title: Docker Sandboxes
description: DockerSandboxManager (create/exec/cleanup), Dockerfiles for CLI/browser/computer_use, bash_exec tool, browser_* tools via Playwright, computer_use_* tools, sandbox inheritance enforcement.

title: DockerSandboxManager
description: nightowl/sandbox/manager.py: create_container(session_id, sandbox_mode) builds/runs appropriate Docker image with CPU/mem limits, no network default. exec_command(container_id, command) runs command and returns stdout/stderr. cleanup(container_id) stops+removes. extract_files(container_id, paths) copies files out.

title: Sandbox Dockerfiles
description: nightowl/sandbox/images/: cli.Dockerfile (Ubuntu + common tools), browser.Dockerfile (cli base + headless Chromium + Playwright), computer_use.Dockerfile (full desktop env + Xvfb + VNC server for screen capture).

title: bash_exec tool
description: nightowl/sandbox/bash_tool.py: Pydantic AI tool. bash_exec(ctx, command) -> runs via DockerSandboxManager.exec_command(). Returns stdout/stderr. Timeout handling.

title: Browser tools
description: nightowl/sandbox/browser_tool.py: browser_navigate(ctx, url), browser_interact(ctx, selector, action, value?), browser_screenshot(ctx). All route through Playwright running inside the browser container. Return page snapshots/screenshots.

title: Computer use tools
description: nightowl/sandbox/computer_use_tool.py: computer_use_screenshot(ctx) captures desktop via VNC/screenshot. computer_use_action(ctx, action, coords?, text?) sends mouse/keyboard events. Uses Claude computer use API patterns.

title: Sandbox inheritance enforcement
description: In SessionManager.spawn_child(): if parent.sandbox_mode != NONE, child must also be sandboxed. Network access and host mounts require HITL approval (HIGH/CRITICAL risk).

title: Core Engine
description: Config, DB, app shell, all data models, SessionManager, depth/role resolution, session tools (spawn/list/send), session runner with completion re-injection, prompt builder for main/orchestrator/leaf roles. This is the heart of NightOwl — blocks everything else.

title: Config + pydantic-settings
description: nightowl/config.py: Settings class with env vars (BEDROCK_REGION, BEDROCK_MODEL, COMPOSIO_API_KEY, TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, TWILIO_*, DATABASE_PATH, MAX_SPAWN_DEPTH=3, MAX_CHILDREN_PER_SESSION=5, HITL_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=120)

comments.author: francois
comments.text: Config (config.py) — pydantic-settings with env vars: Bedrock region/model, Composio key, Telegram/Twilio tokens, PostgreSQL URL, spawn limits (max depth 3, max 5 children, 120s HITL timeout).

title: SQLite DB setup
description: nightowl/db.py: aiosqlite setup. Tables: sessions, messages, approvals. Schema init on startup.

comments.author: francois
comments.text: Database (db.py) — SQLAlchemy async + asyncpg. Three tables: sessions, messages, approvals. Creates schema on startup via init_db().

title: FastAPI app shell
description: nightowl/main.py: FastAPI app with lifespan context manager (init DB, SessionManager, Composio client, skills). CORS middleware. Mount all routers.

comments.author: francois
comments.text: FastAPI Shell (main.py) — Lifespan initialises DB + SessionManager + broadcast queue. CORS middleware. Health endpoint at /api/v1/health.

title: Data models
description: nightowl/models/: Session, SessionRole, SessionState, SandboxMode, SpawnRequest, Message, ChannelMessage, TaskCompletionEvent, RiskLevel, ToolCallWithRisk, ApprovalRequest, ApprovalResponse, AgentCard, IntentNode, IntentEdge, IntentGraph.

comments.author: francois
comments.text: Data Models (models/) — session.py, message.py, approval.py, observability.py. Session, SessionRole, SessionState, SandboxMode, SpawnRequest, TaskCompletionEvent, Message, ChannelMessage, RiskLevel, ToolCallWithRisk, ApprovalRequest/Response, AgentCard, IntentNode, IntentEdge, IntentGraph.

title: SessionManager
description: nightowl/sessions/manager.py: Core orchestrator. create_main_session(), spawn_child() with depth/children validation, complete_session(), deliver_completion_to_parent() with untrusted result markers, send_to_session(). Sessions held in dict, each has asyncio.Queue for inbound messages.

comments.author: francois
comments.text: SessionManager (sessions/manager.py) — In-memory session store with asyncio.Queue per session. create_main_session, spawn_child (depth/children validation + sandbox inheritance), complete_session, deliver_completion_to_parent (untrusted child output), send_to_session. Emits events to broadcast queue.

title: Depth and role resolution
description: nightowl/sessions/depth.py: resolve_role(depth, max_depth) -> main/orchestrator/leaf. Port of OpenClaw's resolveSubagentRoleForDepth.

comments.author: francois
comments.text: Depth Resolution (sessions/depth.py) — Ported from OpenClaw resolveSubagentRoleForDepth. depth 0 = main, 1..n-1 = orchestrator, n = leaf. Leaf cannot spawn.

title: Session tools
description: nightowl/sessions/tools.py: Pydantic AI tool functions — sessions_spawn(ctx, task, label?, sandbox?), sessions_list(ctx), sessions_send(ctx, session_id, message). All use RunContext[AgentState].

comments.author: francois
comments.text: Session Tools (sessions/tools.py) — Three Pydantic AI tool functions using RunContext[AgentState]: sessions_spawn, sessions_list, sessions_send. Spawn returns immediately with no-poll instruction.

title: Session runner
description: nightowl/sessions/runner.py: Run a Pydantic AI agent session. Build agent via build_agent(). Initial run with agent.run(). After spawning children, enter wait loop on inbound_queue. On all completions received, re-run agent with updated history. Stream tokens to TokenStore. Handle errors/timeouts.

comments.author: francois
comments.text: Session Runner (sessions/runner.py) — Builds BedrockConverseModel agent with role-appropriate tools (leaf gets no spawn). Runs initial message, enters wait loop on inbound queue if children spawned. Each completion re-runs agent with updated history.

title: Prompt builder
description: nightowl/sessions/prompt_builder.py: build_system_prompt(session, skills_prompt?). Main: full identity+skills+session tools+composio+HITL awareness. Orchestrator: minimal + subagent context (task, depth, role, parent, no-poll rule). Leaf: minimal + subagent context + no-spawn restriction.

comments.author: francois
comments.text: Prompt Builder (sessions/prompt_builder.py) — Role-scoped system prompts. Main: full identity + skills + session tools + no-poll rule. Orchestrator: task context + depth + spawn ability. Leaf: task context + no-spawn restriction.

title: Composio + HITL
description: Composio meta-tools (search + execute with risk_level arg), Haiku verification classifier for risk > LOW, HITL gate with dual-delivery to dashboard + last channel, channel inline keyboards for approve/reject.

title: Composio meta-tools
description: nightowl/composio_tools/meta_tools.py: composio_search_tools(ctx, query) searches Composio for tools. composio_execute(ctx, tool_name, params, risk_level, risk_justification) executes with HITL gating. Both are Pydantic AI tools using RunContext[AgentState].

title: Haiku risk verification classifier
description: nightowl/hitl/classifier.py: verify_risk(tool_name, tool_args, self_reported_risk, justification, session_context) -> {verified_risk, reasoning}. Calls Haiku via Bedrock. Only runs when self_reported_risk >= MEDIUM. Fast pass to catch false positives.

title: HITL approval gate
description: nightowl/hitl/gate.py: HITLGate class. request_approval(session_id, tool_name, tool_args, risk_level) creates ApprovalRequest, broadcasts via WebSocket + sends to user's last channel (inline keyboard). Waits on asyncio.Event with timeout. resolve_approval(approval_id, approved) sets the event. First response wins.

title: Channels
description: ChannelBridge ABC, Telegram bridge (python-telegram-bot), WhatsApp bridge (Twilio), SMS bridge (Twilio), channel registry + last-channel tracking.

title: ChannelBridge ABC
description: nightowl/channels/base.py: Abstract base class. start(), send_message(user_id, text), send_approval_request(user_id, approval), normalize_inbound(raw) -> ChannelMessage. Channel registry dict + last_channel tracking per user.

title: Telegram bridge
description: nightowl/channels/telegram.py: TelegramBridge(ChannelBridge). Uses python-telegram-bot. Polling mode for dev. On message: normalize to ChannelMessage, call gateway ingest. send_message sends via Bot API. send_approval_request sends inline keyboard with approve/reject buttons.

title: WhatsApp bridge
description: nightowl/channels/whatsapp.py: WhatsAppBridge(ChannelBridge). Uses Twilio WhatsApp Business API. Webhook ingress. normalize_inbound parses Twilio webhook payload. send_message via Twilio client. send_approval_request via interactive message buttons.

title: SMS bridge
description: nightowl/channels/sms.py: SMSBridge(ChannelBridge). Uses Twilio SMS API. Webhook ingress. normalize_inbound parses Twilio webhook. send_message via Twilio. send_approval_request as text with APPROVE/REJECT reply options.
